hypothesis_en: Mr President, I would first of all like to thank Commissioner Patten for his efforts to be present in this debate, despite the difficulties of the agenda, and I would also like to congratulate the Commission on its diligence in presenting the directives for the conclusion of these agreements on political dialogue and cooperation. I would like to thank the Commissioner for his report, and I would also like to congratulate the Commissioner on the work he has done, and on the speed with which he has negotiated these agreements, which, as Mr O'Brien has just pointed out, constitute for Parliament the pre-session of association agreements. This is not a very frequent case, and with today's debate in the plenary and the subsequent vote a new stage is being opened, which, in my opinion, is characterized by the holding of the next Guadalajara summit. In short, Mr President, the world is big and Latin America is only one part of it, but I think it is very satisfactory to recognize, as the Commissioner has done, that Latin America is still a priority area for the European Union, among other things, because we have common values. On 19 March there was a meeting of a large delegation of Members of this Parliament with representatives of the Latin American Parliament and its President, with representatives of the Andean Parliament and its President, with representatives of the Central American Parliament and its President and with representatives of the Mercosur Parliamentary Committee and its President. At that meeting, conclusions were drawn on how to pave the way for that strategy or that bi-regional strategic association which was established at the previous two summits. It was not necessary to have a wide range of statements and statements, but it was enough to achieve this bi-regional strategic association simply by a series of concrete proposals which we have formulated in a decalogue. Because we must not be mistaken, it has been the political will that has made these relations move in recent years, and that is why we are proposing the creation of a Euro-Latin American Parliamentary Assembly, a bit of the same cut as the one that has been produced in the field of Euro-Mediterranean relations, we are proposing a renewal and updating of the ministerial political dialogue, we are also proposing a concertation in the international forums and in the We are also proposing the signing of a Euro-Latin American Charter for Peace and Security in order to create a forum where we can seriously address security and defence issues, which today do not have a specific forum, and above all we are proposing a renewal of the bi-regional political agenda in order to be able to tackle But, Commissioner, the political dialogue, however flourishing, will end up shrinking and reducing to nothing if we do not go from words to deeds. It is clear that a very clear area in which we can go from words to deeds is that of trade relations, and that is why we are proposing that the Guiana Summit set specific dates for association with the Andean and Central American countries without subjecting them to discriminatory conditions that have not been imposed on other countries or groups of countries. This Parliament understands that progress must be made on the path of integration, but integration can be a consequence of association agreements. We should promote a kind of European-oriented alliance in which we give an institutional dimension and a complete geographical coverage to the relations between the European Union and Latin America. We also understand that we have to promote a whole range of policies in the field of education, culture, vocational training and scientific and technological cooperation. We believe that it should be interpreted with flexibility and incorporated into the Commission's initiatives, but we also understand that this initiative must tend to take advantage of the financial availability provided for by both the European Investment Bank and the Inter-American Development Bank. I would like to say that in order for this message to be effective and to have a multiplier effect, Mr President, it must be restored as soon as possible, and I put this provision on behalf of my political group to consolidate this strategic alliance and that It is a harmonious bond between the European Parliament and the Commission, which is at the origin of so much progress between the European Union and Latin America.
reference_en: thank you President first of all I'd like to thank (2.09) the Commissioner Mr Patten for the efforts he's made to be here in this debate despite his timetabling difficulties and also to set aside any doubt I'd like to congratulate the Commission on its diligence in tabling the directives for the conclusion of these agreements on political dialogue and cooperation and I'm sure that Mr Patten will remember the contacts we had previously with Mr Lamy at the Madrid summit and I'd also like to congratulate the Commission for its diligence and effectiveness and rapidity in negotiating these agreements which as Mr Obiols mentioned are (2.24) a pioneering aspect of the association agreements (1.11) and following the debate and the unanimous vote on these two agreements in the Foreign Affairs Committee which doesn't happen very often and the debate now in plenary we're drawing one stage to a conclusion and opening a new stage looking ahead to the ~Guadahalahara summit as Mr Sa= Patten knows better than anywhe= anyone we are in a complex world where the European Union has its own responsibilities both internally (1.68) when we're talking about the constitution or enlargement and also external responsibilities relations with Russia and the problems we still have in the Balkans (1.02) responsibilities in Central Asia Iran Iraq Afghanistan the problem of the Middle East (1.22) resumption of relations with the USA so in the end it's a big world and Latin America is only one part of it but I believe that it's very satisfactory to recognise that Latin America is now being given priority by the European Union among other things because we have common values and a shared history on the nineteenth of March there was a meeting of a large delegation of Members from this Parliament with representatives of the Latin American Parliament and its President and with representatives of the Andean Parliament and its President representatives of the Central American Parliament and its President and representatives of the parliamentary committee of Mercosur and its President so in the end to sum up it was a meeting of Members of Parliament representing more than one thousand million citizens in Europe and Latin America and we came to some conclusions at that meeting as to how to follow the course towards this regional strategy established at the two previous summits and the Members of Parliament who were at the meeting Mr Obiols and other Members of the House who are present now in the debate we all concluded that we don't need a long list of statements and pronouncements but it was enough to begin the strategic regional association to have simply a series of practical proposals which we would then merge together and I would (1.28) sum this up Commissioner by saying that this will be based on three particular spheres political will and dialogue because we shouldn't fool ourselves it was political will which brought us to this level of relations in the last few years and that is why we are now proposing the creation of a Euro-Latin American parliamentary assembly a (1.18) bit like the assembly in the Euro-Mediterranean relations and we are proposing a renewal of political ministerial dialogue and we are also proposing consultation among international fora in the context of the UN system between the positions of Latin American and European positions and we are proposing the signature of a Euro-Latin American Charter for peace and security with a view to creating a forum in which we can tackle seriously the questions concerning security and defence which don't have any specific forum today and above all we are proposing a renewal of the regional political agenda so that we can have a discussion on matters referring to terrorism for example the reform of the UN system the threats to governability (2.71) revision in the fight against poverty and many other significant factors that Commissioner political dialogue however it blossoms will r= be reduced to nothing and will wither away unless we move from words to action it is very clear that one clear area where we can take action is in the area of trade relations and that is why we are proposing that at the ~Guadahalahara summit precise dates be adopted for association with the Latin American and Andean countries without imposing discriminatory conditions upon them which have not been imposed upon other regions or groups of countries (1.37) if we are going to for example integration being a previous condition or whether it should be a consequence of association this Parliament thinks that we need to make progress towards integration but integration can be a consequence of the association agreements and we understand and share your desire that negotiations with Mercosur the negotiation of a balanced agreement can be brought about before the end of the year and this Parliament also understands that as regards the bilateral and regional agreements which have been negotiated on or are being negotiated on we ought to encourage some sort of institutional relationship with the EU and complete geographical coverage for Latin American-EU relations we also understand however that we have to have a cooperation policy which can somehow be adapted to the new concept the new idea of association and in this way you know what Parliament's views are Parliament has stressed that we need a differentiated legal framework and we also understand that we should encourage a whole series of policies under education culture vocational training (1.24) scientific and technological cooperation and you also know Commissioner that Parliament has proposed the creation of a solidarity a regional solidarity fund an initiative which we for which we do not need extra bureaucratic structures this can be interpreted with flexibility but we also think that this initiative should be able to take advantage of the financial availabilities from the European Investment Bank and Bank and the Inter-American Development Bank so Commissioner I just wanted to say that as you see the Parliament's proposals are very ambitious we hope that the ambition will be shared by the Member States and the Commission (1.17) in the communication it's preparing so that we can send out a clear well-defined message regarding a new commitment of Europe to Latin ~American and for this message to be effective and to have a great impact I'm about to conclude President for the message to be clear and effective (1.02) we need to restore as soon as possible President (1.84) and my political group is aware is available to do this so that we can support the strategic alliance between the European Parliament and the Commission which is the source of so much progress between the Euro= EU and Latin America thank you
source_es: (1.42) sí gracias señor Presidente yo quisiera en primer lugar agradecer los esfuerzos que me consta ha hecho el Comisario el Señor Patten para estar presente en este debate a pesar de las dificultades de agenda y también quisiera para disipar cualquier duda felicitar a la Comisión por su diligencia en la presentación de las directivas para la conclusión de estos Acuerdos de Diálogo Político y Cooperación y estoy seguro que el Señor Patten recordará los contactos que mantuvimos con carácter previo también con el Señor Lamy a la Cumbre de Madrid y felicitar también a la Comisión por la diligencia y por la eficacia y por la rapidez en la negociación de estos Acuerdos que como acaba de recordar el Señor Obiols constituyen para el Parlamento precisamente la antesala de acuerdos de asociación precisamente con la discusión en la Comisión de Asuntos Exteriores y el voto que fue un voto unánime de estos dos acuerdos caso que no es muy frecuente y con el debate hoy en el Pleno y el voto subsiguiente se cierra una etapa y se abre una nueva etapa que viene en mi opinión caracterizada por la celebración de la próxima Cumbre de Guadalajara como el Señor Patten sabe mejor que nadie vivimos en un mundo complejo donde la Unión Europea tiene responsabilidades que se plantean tanto en el ámbito interno como el alumbramiento de la Constitución o la ampliación y también responsabilidades externas la nueva relación con Rusia los problemas que todavía seguimos teniendo en los Balcanes las responsabilidades en Asia Central con Irán Iraq Afganistán el problema de Oriente Medio el rediseñar nuevas relaciones con los Estados Unidos en definitiva señor Presidente el mundo es grande y América Latina es sólo una parte pero yo creo que es muy satisfactorio reconocer como ha hecho el Comisario que América Latina sigue siendo un área prioritaria para la Unión Europea entre otras cosas porque tenemos unos valores comunes y una historia compartida (1.13) el pasado diecinueve de marzo hubo una reunión de una numerosa delegación de miembros de este Parlamento con representantes del Parlamento latinoamericano y su Presidente con representantes del Parlamento andino y su Presidente con representantes del Parlamento centroamericano y su Presidente y con representantes de la comisión parlamentaria del Mercosur y con su Presidente también en definitiva en resumen una reunión de parlamentarios que representaban a más de mil millones de ciudadanos de Europa y de América Latina y en esa reunión se establecieron unas conclusiones de cómo jalonar el camino hacia esa estrategia o a esa asociación estratégica birregional que se estableció en las dos Cumbres anteriores y los parlamentarios que estuvimos en esa reunión estuvo el Señor Obiols y otros miembros de esta Cámara que están presentes en este debate concluimos (1.07) que no hacía falta un amplio catálogo de declaraciones y de pronunciamientos sino que bastaba para alcanzar esa asociación estratégica birregional simplemente una serie de proposiciones concretas que nosotros plasmamos en un decálogo y yo le resumiría señor Comisario fundamentalmente ese decálogo está basado en tres ámbitos en el ámbito de la voluntad y del diálogo político porque no debemos engañarnos ha sido la voluntad política la que ha hecho moverse estas relaciones en los últimos años y por eso estamos proponiendo la creación de una Asamblea parlamentaria eurolatinoamericana un poco del mismo corte que la Asamblea que se ha producido en el ámbito de las relaciones euromediterráneas estamos proponiendo una renovación y puesta al día del diálogo político ministerial estamos proponiendo también una concertación en los foros internacionales en el ámbito del sistema de Naciones Unidas entre las posiciones de europeos y latinoamericanos y estamos proponiendo también la firma de una Carta Eurolatinoamericana para la paz y la seguridad al objeto de crear un foro donde podamos abordar seriamente las cuestiones relativas a la seguridad y la defensa que hoy por hoy no tienen un foro específico y sobre todo estamos proponiendo una renovación de la agenda política birregional para poder acometer la discusión de asuntos como los que se refieren al f= fenómeno terrorista al tema relativo a la reforma del sistema de Naciones Unidas a las amenazas a la gobernabilidad a la revisión en el ámbito de la lucha contra la pobreza y muchas otras iniciativas pero señor Comisario el diálogo político por muy floreciente que sea acabará agostándose y reduciéndose a la nada si no pasamos de las palabras a los hechos y es evidente que un ámbito muy claro en el que podemos pasar de las palabras a los hechos es en el de las relaciones comerciales y por eso estamos proponiendo el que en la Cumbre de Guadalajara se señalen fechas concretas para la asociación con los países andinos y centroamericanos sin someterlas a condiciones discriminatorias que no se hayan impuesto a otros países o grupos de países y aquí hay un debate usted se ha referido a la integración sobre la sinte= sobre si la integración tiene que ser una condición previa o si la integración es una consecuencia de la asociación este Parlamento entiende que efectivamente hay que hacer progresos en la vía de la integración pero la integración puede ser una consecuencia de los acuerdos de asociación entendemos también y compartimos su deseo de que las negociaciones con el Mercosur la negociación de un acuerdo equilibrado pueda culminarse antes de finales del presente año y este Parlamento entiende también que en respeto a los acuerdos bilaterales y regionales que ya han sido negociados o que están en curso de negociación deberíamos promover una especie de ALCA con alma a la europea en la que diésemos una dimensión institucional y una cobertura geográfica completa a las relaciones entre la Unión Europea y América Latina y entendemos también señor Comisario que tenemos que tener una política de cooperación que pueda de alguna manera adaptarse a este nuevo concepto a esta nueva noción de la asociación (1.05) y en este sentido usted conoce cuáles son las tesis del Parlamento el Parlamento ha insistido por tener un marco jurídico diferenciado y entendemos también que tenemos que promover toda una serie de políticas en el ámbito de la educación en el ámbito de la cultura en el ámbito de la formación profesional y en el ámbito de la cooperación científica y tecnológica usted sabe señor Comisario que el Parlamento ha propuesto la constitución de un fondo de ~solidarid birregional una iniciativa en la que nosotros no queremos crear estructuras burocráticas suplementarias creemos que tiene que ser interpretada con flexibilidad e insertarse en las iniciativas de la Comisión pero entendemos también que esta iniciativa debe tender a aprovechar las ~disposinibilidades financieras que prevén tanto el Banco Europeo de Inversiones como el Banco Interamericano de Desarrollo por último señor Comisario quisiera decirle que como ve usted hay una gran ambición en las propuestas de los Parlamentos esperamos que esa ambición pueda ser compartida por los Estados miembros y por la Comisión de la comunicación que está preparando de tal forma que se pueda lanzar un mensaje claro y bien definido del nuevo compromiso de Europa con América Latina y quisiera decirle que para que ese mensaje sea eficaz y pueda tener un efecto multiplicador voy a terminar ya señor Presidente para que ese mensaje sea eficaz y tenga un efecto multiplicador hay que restaurar lo antes posible señor Presidente y yo pongo esa disposición en nombre de mi grupo político para que se consolide esa alianza estratégica y ese tandem armónico entre el Parlamento Europeo y la Comisión que está en el origen de tantos progresos entre la Unión Europea y América Latina muchas gracias señor Presidente
